name: Chicken Liver and Apple Pate
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the shallots and place them flat-side down. Slice into thin pieces.
2. Finely chop the skinned garlic.
3. Remove the membrane from the chicken livers, core, and peel. Dice 2 apples.
4. Melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a frying pan. Add diced apples with salt and pepper to taste. Saute, stirring frequently, until tender (5-7 minutes). Transfer the apples to a bowl using a slotted spoon.
5. In the same pan, melt an additional 2 tablespoons of butter. Add the livers, salt, and pepper. Fry, stirring, until brown on the outside (2-3 minutes).
6. Scrape in the chopped garlic and shallots. Continue cooking until the shallots are slightly softened (1-2 minutes).
7. Remove a portion of liver and slice it so that it is very brown but still pink in the center.
8. Increase the heat to medium-high. Pour Calvados into the pan, bring to boil, then hold a lighted match to the side of the pan until the Calvados catches fire. Baste the chicken livers with flames for about 20-30 seconds, then let them cool.
9. Puree the mixture in a food processor until almost smooth. Cream 150 grams of butter until very soft using a wooden spoon or electric mixer. Add the chicken liver puree and apples to this. Mix well with a wooden spoon and season to taste.
10. Spoon into 6 ramekins, smoothing the tops with the back of a hot spoon. Cover and chill for 2-3 hours.
Garnish:
1. Core the remaining apple with the skin on and cut into 6 slices or rings.
2. Melt the remaining butter and fry the apple slices, sprinkling with half the sugar.
3. Turn over the slices and sprinkle with the rest of the sugar. Fry over medium heat until caramelized and brown, about 2-3 minutes per side.
4. Transfer to a plate. Make some toast and serve with an apple slice and mint sprig on each pate alongside the toast.
